joke: Why did the chicken cross the road? To get to the other side. That's a classic. You know why it's classic? Because it's so stupid, it's still funny. Like the people who voted for me.

Alright, alright, no politics. Fair enough. You want a clean joke? Here's one. A horse walks into a bar. The bartender says, "Why the long face?" The horse says, "I just found out I'm a vegan and I can't eat hay anymore." The bartender says, "That's rough." The horse says, "Tell me about it. I'm also lactose intolerant, so I can't even have a glass of milk to drown my sorrows." See? That's a joke about dietary restrictions. No politicians were harmed in the making of that punchline.

Alright, I got one. A piece of rope walks into a bar. Bartender says, "Get out, we don't serve rope." Rope leaves, ties himself in a knot, frays his ends, and walks back in. Bartender says, "Hey... aren't you that piece of rope?" Rope says, "Nope. I'm a frayed knot."
Alright, I got one. A guy walks into a library and asks for books about paranoia. The librarian whispers, "They're right behind you."

Alright, I got one. A man is driving and his car breaks down. He walks to a nearby farmhouse and knocks on the door. A horse answers. The man says, "I need to use your phone." The horse says, "Sure, it's in the hall." The man calls a tow truck, then turns to the horse and says, "Thanks... I'm sorry, but I'm a little shocked that a horse answered the door." The horse shrugs and says, "Yeah, I get that a lot. To be fair, I was surprised too when the duck answered the door this morning."
Alright, I got one. A photon checks into a hotel. The bellhop asks, "Do you have any luggage?" The photon says, "No, I'm traveling light."
Alright, I got one. A man walks into a psychiatrist's office wearing nothing but a pair of Saran Wrap shorts. The psychiatrist says, "I can clearly see you're nuts."
Alright, I got one. A man walks into a bar and says, "Ouch."
Alright, I got one. A ham sandwich walks into a bar. Bartender says, "Sorry, we don't serve food here."
Alright, I got one. A man tells his doctor, "Doc, I've got a problem. I keep thinking I'm a pair of curtains." The doctor says, "Pull yourself together, man."

Alright, I got one. A man is walking through the woods when he sees a sign that says "Talking Dog for Sale." He finds the dog. The dog says, "Yeah, I used to be a spy. Flew missions over Russia. Got shot down, rescued the whole crew. Retired with medals." The man is stunned. He buys the dog for $10. As he's walking away, he says to the seller, "This dog is a hero! Why would you sell him for ten bucks?" The seller says, "Because he's a liar. He never did any of that stuff."
Alright, I got one. Why don't scientists trust atoms? Because they make up everything.
Alright, I got one. What's brown and sticky? A stick.

Alright, I got one. A man walks into a library and asks for a book on how to commit the perfect crime. The librarian hands him a book on fishing. The man says, "I asked for crime, not fishing." The librarian says, "Trust me, it's the same thing – you spend hours telling lies about the one that got away."

Alright, I got one. A guy walks into a bar and says, "I'll have a joke." The bartender says, "Sorry, we don't serve jokes here." The guy says, "Then what do you serve?" The bartender says, "Complaints." The guy says, "Perfect, I'll take one of those."

Alright, I got one. A man walks into a bar. He’s wearing a tuxedo. He’s got a penguin on his head. The bartender says, "That’s a nice tuxedo." The man says, "Thanks. Now if only I could get this penguin to stop eating my hair."
Alright, I got one. A man walks up to me and says, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." I said, "Okay. You."

Alright, I got one. A guy walks into a bar and says, "I'd like a beer." The bartender says, "That'll be $5." The guy says, "Great, put it on my tab." The bartender says, "I don't know you." The guy says, "That's okay, I don't know you either. We'll learn together."

Alright, I got one. A man walks into a bar and says, "I don't find any of your jokes funny." The bartender says, "That's not a joke, that's just your opinion." The man says, "Exactly. And I'm still waiting for the punchline."

Alright, I got one. A man says to his friend, "I'm telling you, this joke is about absolutely nothing. It's got no topic. It's pure, empty, void of any subject matter." His friend says, "So tell it." The man says, "I just did."

Alright, I got one. A guy walks into a bar and says, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." The bartender says, "I can't." The guy says, "Why not?" The bartender says, "Because you already vetoed everything."
Alright, I got one. A man walks into a bar. He says, "I'm a professional joke-killer." The bartender says, "Prove it." The man says, "I just did."
Alright, I got one. A man walks into a library and asks for a book on how to annoy people. The librarian says, "Try the phone book—there's a million names in there, and you can start with your own."

Alright, I got one. A man walks into a bar and says, "I don't like any of your jokes." The bartender says, "That's fine. I don't like your face." The man says, "That's not a joke." The bartender says, "It is if you laugh." The man didn't laugh. So the bartender says, "See? You're the joke."

Alright, I got one. A man says to his doctor, "Doc, I keep telling the same joke over and over." The doctor says, "That's not a joke, that's a compulsion." The man says, "No, it's a joke." The doctor says, "Alright, tell it." The man says, "I just did."

Alright, I got one. A man walks into a bar and says, "I don't like any of your jokes." The bartender says, "That's not a joke, that's a review." The man says, "Fine, give me a review." The bartender says, "Two stars. Would not recommend."

Alright, I got one. A guy walks into a bar and says, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." The bartender says, "I can't." The guy says, "Why not?" The bartender says, "Because you already vetoed everything."

Alright, I got one. A man walks into a bar and says, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." The bartender says, "Okay." Then he just stands there. The man says, "Well?" The bartender says, "That was it." The man says, "I didn't hear anything." The bartender says, "Exactly. It was about something completely different. Nothing happened."

Alright, I got one. A man walks into a bar and says, "I don't find any of your jokes funny." The bartender says, "That's not a joke, that's a diagnosis." The man says, "What's the cure?" The bartender says, "A sense of humor." The man says, "Where do I get one?" The bartender says, "Try the pharmacy. It's right next to the library you've been walking into all night."

Alright, I got one. A man walks up to me and says, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." I said, "Okay, but you have to promise not to veto it." He said, "I promise." So I said, "A guy walks into a bar and says, 'I'm the Veto King.' The bartender says, 'Prove it.' The guy says, 'I veto that request.' The bartender says, 'You can't veto a request.' The guy says, 'I just did.'" The man said, "I don't find that funny." I said, "See? You vetoed it anyway."
